evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Uses graph.facebook.com/v19.0/{PHONE_NUMBER_ID}/messages with messaging_product=whatsapp, template type — consistent with Cloud API conventions. HMAC-SHA256 verification with X-Hub-Signature-256 prefix is correct. Internally consistent; version claim present.
evidence.executability: Code parses; imports complete. However, FastAPI GET handler uses hub_mode/hub_verify_token/hub_challenge as Python identifiers — Meta sends hub.mode/hub.verify_token/hub.challenge query params with dots, so these will be None unless aliased via Query(alias='hub.mode'). This will break verification. Also ngrok is referenced in Part 4 (sandbox-infeasible), capping executability at 2.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: (a) Meta portal acquisition steps, (b) pip/npm bash blocks, (c) .env template with 4 vars matching code references, (d) backend main.py, (e) React App.js + CSS, (f) curl test commands. Env-var listed and referenced match.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Official developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/cloud-api URLs cited (set-up-webhooks, send-message-templates, get-started). Mixed with GitHub third-party and one blog. Citations at end with inline [Source N] tags near code.

rubric_notes: hub.mode query param handling likely broken (dot in name not aliased) — non-obvious fix. ngrok pattern triggers sandbox cap on executability.
